structural_claim: Measurement fundamentally alters the system being measured, selecting from multiple possible outcomes rather than revealing pre-existing properties

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Quantum observables cannot possess predetermined values independent of measurement context
Frequent measurements can freeze quantum evolution, revealing measurement as an active process that fundamentally alters system dynamics

Quark distributions in nucleons are modified when bound in nuclei, revealing that nuclear structure emerges from QCD degrees of freedom and that nucleons lose their individual identity in the nuclear medium
